title: Robopsychology: A Framework for Behavioral Diagnostics of AI Systems
description:
Robopsychology is a behavioral diagnostic framework for large language model systems. It separates any observed output into three layers — model tendencies, runtime/host pressure, and conversation effects — and provides sixteen diagnostic prompts named after patterns in Asimov's robot stories, five operating rules, and a nine-step diagnostic ratchet that makes performed transparency expensive and genuine transparency cheap.

This deposit archives the framework documents under framework/, the modular prompt cards and prompt catalog under prompts/, no-CLI diagnostic templates under templates/, the reproducible validation cases, and the robopsych reference CLI implementation. The canonical statement of the framework is the essay Robopsychology.

The accompanying paper draft (paper/) is included for transparency but is not yet submission-ready; quantitative validation of the ratchet across model families is an active research track tracked in the repository's issue queue.
